author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Separate in-flow bounds from scrollable overflow

Track in-flow content independently when adding a scroll container’s
end padding. Out-of-flow boxes and propagated visible overflow have
already extended the scrollable overflow area and must not receive the
container padding a second time.

Add coverage for positioned content at and beyond the padding edge, and
correct the existing in-flow padding expectation.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Preserve text input size after table normalization

Use the text control default preferred size when table display
normalization causes an appearance:none input to enter replaced sizing.
Otherwise its automatic height falls through to the generic 150px
replaced-element fallback.

Add regression coverage comparing normalized table-cell and block text
inputs.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI/Qt: Cascade popups without explicit coordinates

Use the regular new-window cascade position for each coordinate that
window.open() leaves unspecified. Preserve explicitly requested popup
coordinates and existing popup sizing behavior.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI/Qt: Recreate web view when detaching tabs

Notify the QRhiWidget that its window is changing before destroying its
native surface. This releases its resources and unregisters the cleanup
callback from the source window RHI before that window is destroyed.

Recreate the surface after adoption and keep new detached windows hidden
until the recreated view is installed, letting Qt configure the backing
store before first exposure.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI/Qt: Open external URLs outside private windows

Route QFileOpenEvent requests to a non-private browser window.
Use the active normal window first, then reuse any normal window. Create
one when only private windows exist.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI/Qt: Focus web view after activating bookmarks

Return focus to the active tab after a bookmark action starts loading.
This ends omnibox editing and keeps the bookmark URL visible. Bookmark
actions from both the menu and bookmarks bar share this path.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Clear text selection after clicking within it

Preserve an existing selection on mouse-down so macOS can begin a text
drag or dictionary lookup. When an uncancelled mouseup occurs at the
original position, clear a document selection or collapse an editable
selection to the clicked caret position.

This follows Blink and WebKit for non-editable content. Gecko likewise
defers the selection change until mouseup, but collapses non-editable
selections to a caret.

Cover completed primary clicks and cancelled mouseup events.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Resolve user-select values without layout nodes

Resolve user-select used values from DOM computed styles and style
inheritance ancestry so display: contents elements participate in the
selection algorithm even though they do not generate layout nodes.

Use DOM boundary points when clamping selections. A boxless ancestor can
otherwise turn a pre-order position before a control into one after it.

Cover none, all, contain, nested auto, controls, and slotted content
across boxless ancestors.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI/Qt: Offset native macOS window controls

AppKit lays out the standard window controls after Qt creates the native
surface, leaving them misaligned with Ladybird's taller tab strip.

Observe frame changes from the native titlebar layout and reapply a
six-point offset after AppKit finishes laying out the controls.

author: Aliaksandr Kalenik
message:
LibWeb: Honor IDL nonces on inline style elements

Style element CSP checks required the nonce content attribute before
comparing its internal value. Unlike scripts, Chromium and Gecko pass
a style element's internal nonce directly to CSP, which matters when
script sets HTMLStyleElement.nonce because that setter intentionally
does not create a content attribute.

Restrict this interoperability behavior to HTML and SVG style elements
while retaining nonceability checks for scripts. Add a regression test
for a dynamically inserted style whose nonce is set through the IDL
property.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Store Web platform strings as UTF-16

Move DOM and HTML text, Web-facing APIs, parser and URL plumbing, CSS
and ARIA serialization, generated bindings, and IPC boundaries to
UTF-16.

Add borrowed UTF-16 paths where ownership is unnecessary, keep byte
conversions explicit at system boundaries, and update tests and
utilities for the new representation.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI: Distinguish profiles with Dock icon badges

Share one Cocoa badge renderer between the Qt and AppKit frontends on
macOS. Keep the standard icon for the default profile and overlay a
larger badge on other profiles using a curated Ladybird-adjacent color
palette and up to the first three characters of the profile name.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Place text control carets from padding clicks

Google's search field uses a one-line textarea with generous padding.
Clicking that padding left the caret unchanged because the initial hit
test did not resolve against the text in its user-agent shadow tree.

Constrain initial caret hit testing to a focused text control's internal
selection scope. Resolve points beyond a line's inline bounds to the
logical line edge, taking reversed inline axes into account.

Add regression coverage for input and textarea padding on every side in
LTR and RTL controls.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
UI/AppKit: Isolate saved window frames by profile

Key AppKit window frame persistence by the canonical profile identity so
profiles cannot observe each other's saved window positions. Avoid all
window state persistence for temporary profiles.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWebView: Isolate browser state by profile

Select the profile before opening persistent state or launching helpers.
Route settings, bookmarks, databases, HTTP and Alt-Svc caches, and Skia
caches through explicit paths shared with the sandbox allowlists. Keep
Android on its existing private layout without exposing profile CLI.

Use a startup lock and per-profile endpoints to coordinate ownership.
Forward URLs before opening stores. Retain --force-new-process as a
deprecated temporary-profile alias.

Test isolation of settings, bookmarks, databases, and HTTP cache entries
between profiles.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
Meta: Add profile migration tool

Provide a one-time macOS and Linux tool that copies unsuffixed Ladybird
configuration and data into the default profile while leaving the source
intact. Exclude existing profiles and cached data. Refuse to overwrite a
populated destination.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWebView: Add browser profile path resolver

Introduce the final profile layout with a stable named default profile.
Support named, custom-path, and temporary alternatives. Validate each
selector and canonicalize routing identities. Create private directories
and remove temporary profiles when their owner is destroyed.

Cover profile naming, layouts, selector validation, routing identity and
temporary-profile cleanup.

author: Aliaksandr Kalenik
message:
LibWeb: Simplify text fragment iteration over flat inline storage

TextOffsetMapping still searches the paintable subtree for the per-line
inline paintables that used to hold a text node's fragments. All
fragments of a text node now live on its containing block's
PaintableWithLines in line order, so scan that flat list directly.

author: Aliaksandr Kalenik
message:
LibWeb: Store a single paintable pointer on Layout::Node

Every layout node now has at most one paintable, but Layout::Node still
carries a DoublyLinkedList of them, callers still loop over it, and the
absolute_united_* rect helpers still exist only to merge a fragmented
inline's per-line paintables into one rect.

Collapse the list to a plain RefPtr and delete the united-rect helpers,
which by now return exactly what the plain absolute rect getters return.
No functional change; the full test suite passes without a single
expectation update.

author: Aliaksandr Kalenik
message:
LibWeb: Store inline content flat on the block and add InlinePaintable

A wrapped inline box owns one paintable per line it spans, so
LayoutState::commit() stitches the paint tree together in fragile
order-dependent passes, fragmented inlines never hit the paintable reuse
cache, and a wrapped opacity span composites per line instead of once.

Now the block establishing the inline formatting context owns all its
inline content in flat line-ordered vectors, and each fragmented inline
box becomes a single fragment-less InlinePaintable whose geometry comes
from the pieces computed during inline layout. commit() is reduced to a
plain transfer, and inline paintables become 1:1 with layout nodes and
cacheable like every other paintable.

This fixes bugs the old model could not express, among them sliced edges
on the wrong physical sides in rtl and vertical text, padding never
sliced at wrap boundaries, carets stuck inside positioned inline boxes,
and unclickable empty editable inline boxes. Every behavior change is
pinned by a preparation-commit test or a rebaselined expectation.

author: Aliaksandr Kalenik
message:
LibWeb: Compute inline box pieces during inline layout

Which sides of a wrapped inline box's border and padding appear on each
line is guessed at LayoutState::commit() time, by comparing which layout
node ends one line and starts the next. The guess breaks when a box's
content spans several text nodes or is interrupted by a block-in-inline,
and it cannot place sliced edges on the correct physical sides for rtl
and vertical writing modes.

Inline layout is where a box's true first and last content fragment are
known, so compute one InlineBoxPiece per fragmented box and line there,
after all fragment-mutating line passes: a border-box rect plus the
physical edges that piece carries. The output is stored on the
formatting context root and is unused for now; the upcoming rework
switches painting over to it.

author: Aliaksandr Kalenik
message:
LibWeb: Prepare paint helpers and tests for inline fragment rework

Backgrounds, borders, box shadows and outlines can only be painted at a
paintable's own absolute rect, but the upcoming rework of fragmented
inline boxes needs to paint each of them once per line piece. Split the
paint helpers into rect-parameterized variants now, so the rework does
not have to duplicate their logic.

Behavior around fragmented inlines (hit testing, carets, client rects,
stacking contexts, border slicing, visibility) is also barely covered by
tests, so the rework could regress it silently. Pin the current behavior
down with regression tests first, so the rework's intentional changes
surface as reviewable expectation updates. No behavior change in this
commit.

author: sideshowbarker
message:
Docs: Clarify note about Kitware’s apt repository

Fixes https://github.com/LadybirdBrowser/ladybird/issues/10402

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Dispatch form control events synchronously

Blink, WebKit, and Gecko notify script in the same stack after a user
interaction changes a form control. Queuing these events lets controlled
components restore stale state before observing the new value.

Dispatch input synchronously for all user-driven input value changes.
Send commit-time change and file picker cancel notifications in the same
stack. Keep intentionally scheduled selection events unchanged.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Test form control event ordering

Model controlled range and text controls that render their last
event-observed values immediately after user interaction. This captures
the remaining delayed input notifications without timing dependencies.

Also cover change delivery when a textarea loses focus.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Dispatch text control input events synchronously

HTML specifies queued input event delivery for text controls, but Blink,
WebKit, and Gecko dispatch the event synchronously after each edit.
Deferring it lets a controlled component restore stale state before its
input handler observes the newest value, which can swallow rapid edits.

Dispatch input synchronously for input and textarea text editing while
leaving other form control interactions queued. Update event ordering
expectations and cover the controlled-render scenario without timing.

author: Andreas Kling
message:
LibWeb: Test text control input event ordering

Model a controlled text input that renders its last event-observed value
immediately after an edit. This deterministically captures the delayed
input notification without relying on timers or scheduling delays.

Cover input and textarea controls, including the observable ordering
of beforeinput, textInput, and input.

author: sideshowbarker
message:
Tests: Drop unstable counters from :has() sibling combinator test

Problem: The has-sibling-combinator-non-subject.html test occasionally
fails on CI in the first case (“adjacent match stops after plain element
inserted between operands”). styleInvalidations drifts from the expected
3 up to 5 under concurrent test scheduling. Cases 2–24 are stable.

Cause: The styleInvalidations counter is cache-warmth-dependent for
the first case in the run — same pattern PR #9642 addressed for the
has-concrete-structural-triggers.

Fix: Add an omitAllCounters per-case option to the runSiblingHasCase
helper, and opt the first case in. Drop the counter trailer from that
case’s PASS line; the RESULT line preceding it still records initial/
after/expected — so the test still verifies correctness. It just no
longer over-specifies counts that are cache-warmth-dependent.

author: Jelle Raaijmakers
message:
LibWeb: Prevent quadratic insertion performance in parser

The parser was calling into `TreeNode::child_count()` in order to append
new nodes, but we don't need to do that - we can use a sentinel value to
indicate we want to append and modify the behavior of `NodeAndOffset`
slightly so it returns the expected values in its API.

This results in us being able to run WPT's encoding tests significantly
faster; I'm seeing upwards of 10x runtime reductions on specific
`encoding` tests on my machine.

author: Jelle Raaijmakers
message:
Tests/LibWeb: Make `setTestTimeout()` always set a new timeout

The old behavior only set a new timeout if it was larger than the
configured test timeout. This is both pretty confusing for an API called
`setTestTimeout()` _and_ was already causing no-ops for tests trying to
reduce their timeouts.

The new behavior always restarts the timeout timer and allows tests to
either extend or reduce their timeout. This does mean that tests could
repeatedly call this to indefinitely extend their timeout, but it seems
unlikely that we would write and accept such a test, and the overall
testing and/or job timeout will still catch such a mistake at the end.
